title: COMPAT — NAICS 221114 Solar Electric Power Generation

sections.title: Summary
sections.content:
| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| Constraints assessed | 9 |
| knockout | **0** |
| mitigable | 4 |
| none | 5 |
| Market verdict | **SURVIVING** — no knockouts |
| Aggregate mitigation cost | low–medium |
| Rationale | PV+BESS co-location is industry-standard; Mein Solar's PV-EPC incumbency and VDE-AR-N 4110 / FGW TR8 path map 1:1 to this market |

---

sections.title: Verdict table
sections.content:
| # | Constraint | Type | is_abs | Threshold | Verdict | Cost | Time |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | MV Grid Connection Capacity | operational | false | — | **mitigable** | medium | 6–18 mo |
| 2 | Land Footprint and Siting | physical | false | 40–80 m²/MWh | **none** | — | — |
| 3 | Fire-Protection Clearance | regulatory | false | per VdS / Feuerwehr | **mitigable** | low | 1–2 mo |
| 4 | Li-Ion Thermal Runaway Hazard | physical | false | — | **mitigable** | low | in-design |
| 5 | Grid Code Certification Lead Time | regulatory | false | 6–12 months | **mitigable** | low | 6–12 mo |
| 6 | Battery Supply Chain Concentration | economic | false | — | **none** | — | — |
| 7 | Ambient Temperature Envelope | environmental | false | -20 to +50 °C | **none** | — | — |
| 8 | Round-Trip Energy Losses | physical | true | 10–15 % | **none** | — | — |
| 9 | CAPEX Viability Threshold | economic | false | €250–400/kWh | **none** | — | — |

No constraint is a physics-knockout against this market. Cost constraints are never knockouts. All regulatory gates have certification paths that PV-EPCs already walk daily.

---

sections.title: Per-constraint rationale
sections.content:
### 1. MV Grid Connection Capacity — **mitigable**

| | |
|---|---|
| Rationale | Solar parks ARE MV-connected by definition (same VDE-AR-N 4110 regime). A PV+BESS retrofit may need DSO capacity upgrade or transformer resizing when BESS is added behind an existing PV connection point. |
| Mitigation actions | Early DSO capacity study; share the existing PV grid-connection point (netzdienliche Kopplung); negotiate capacity upgrade; design BESS to match existing Ausbaustufe. |
| Cost | medium (€10K–100K DSO study + cost-allocation negotiation) |
| Time | 6–18 months on critical path; parallelizable with EPC design |

### 2. Land Footprint and Siting — **none**

| | |
|---|---|
| Rationale | Utility-scale PV parks occupy 1.5–2.5 ha/MWp. A 9 MWh BESS (40–80 m²/MWh ≈ 360–720 m²) consumes <1% of an existing PV site. Land is already secured, fenced, and permitted. |

### 3. Fire-Protection Clearance — **mitigable**

| | |
|---|---|
| Rationale | Solar parks are rural, low-density sites — distance-to-property-line and Feuerwehr-Zufahrt are routinely achievable. For >3 MWh container clusters a VdS-aligned separation plan is standard. |
| Mitigation actions | VdS 6023/3103 alignment; container spacing ≥3 m; deflagration venting; dedicated access road; Feuerwehr Einsatzplan. |
| Cost | low (<€10K incremental vs. baseline BESS design) |
| Time | 1–2 months in design phase |

### 4. Li-Ion Thermal Runaway Hazard — **mitigable**

| | |
|---|---|
| Rationale | Inherent chemistry risk applies to every BESS in every market — cannot be eliminated. Industry-standard containment (NOVEC 1230, compartmentalization, BMS early warning, deflagration venting) is already part of Mein Solar's reference design. No market-specific escalation for PV co-location. |
| Mitigation actions | Grade-A LFP cells only; NOVEC 1230 suppression; cell-level temperature monitoring via BMS; compartmentalized racks; deflagration panels. |
| Cost | low (baseline cost of any BESS EPC, already in €/kWh) |
| Time | in-design |

### 5. Grid Code Certification Lead Time — **mitigable**

| | |
|---|---|
| Rationale | Solar parks need identical FGW TR3 (unit) + TR8 (plant type B) certification against VDE-AR-N 4110. Mein Solar's PV-EPC practice already runs this process. Lead time is parallel to procurement/construction, not additive. |
| Mitigation actions | Kick off grid-study at contract signing; use pre-certified container from Tier-1 L2 supplier (CATL EnerC / Sungrow / SolarEast); engage FGW body (TÜV NORD / 8.2) early; bundle with existing PV plant cert where co-located. |
| Cost | low (€5K–€30K certification-body fees; already in EPC scope) |
| Time | 6–12 months parallel |

### 6. Battery Supply Chain Concentration — **none**

| | |
|---|---|
| Rationale | FX, tariff, and CRMA exposure apply to every BESS project across every market — not market-specific. PV+BESS customers already accept this reality as a system-level procurement risk. Cost-class, not market-class, constraint. |

### 7. Ambient Temperature Envelope — **none**

| | |
|---|---|
| Rationale | German solar park sites sit well inside the -20 to +50 °C envelope. Historical record in DE Solarpark-Standorten: -18 °C to +38 °C. HVAC maintains internal setpoint with roundtrip-efficiency penalty <2% at extremes. No design escalation required. |

### 8. Round-Trip Energy Losses — **none**

| | |
|---|---|
| Rationale | 10–15% AC-AC loss is absolute (thermodynamic floor), but it applies equally to every BESS in every market. PV+BESS business case is built on this loss assumption — revenue-stack models (arbitrage, peak shaving, §19 StromNEV) already price it in. Constraint irrelevant as a market gate. |

### 9. CAPEX Viability Threshold — **none**

| | |
|---|---|
| Rationale | PV+BESS market is the single most CAPEX-mature BESS segment — €250–400/kWh band is achieved routinely by Tier-1 L2 suppliers (CATL EnerC, Sungrow, SolarEast). Solar developers already underwrite 3–5 yr payback models on this CAPEX. Economics are proven, not a barrier. Cost is never a knockout per step-08 rules. |

---

sections.title: Conservative review (08b)
sections.content:
All 4 mitigable verdicts reviewed for upgrade risk → all remain mitigable.
No knockouts exist → nothing to downgrade.
Two-question test N/A (no knockouts).

**Market 221114 → surviving. Enters Step 09 feature-market fit assessment with no carried penalties.**

---
